pages.text: Flue curing uses controlled heat rather than open smoke. The idea is to dry the leaf with warmth while avoiding direct smoke contact. This method is strongly associated with brighter, sweeter, and more refined tobacco styles.

If your modpack or future versions of the mod support heat-based curing, flue curing should be treated as a carefully managed process. Too little heat may slow the cure or leave the leaf under-processed. Too much heat may damage the tobacco and lower quality.

Flue-cured leaf is often valuable in cigarettes and brighter blends because it can preserve sugars and produce a smoother, lighter smoke. It is less rustic than fire curing and more controlled than open-air methods.
When adding or tuning flue curing mechanics, focus on stable heat, steady progress, and clear penalties for overheating. It should feel precise rather than crude.
